title: Cycle 87 Proof

Cycle86 still seems existential rather than replayable: the candidate relies on a counted good translate/cubic. I’m checking the six-jet shortening, transversality, and separator-wall compatibility.

Quotient structure is safe because the census projection deliberately coarsens projective classes. Periodic/tuple coincidences are retained rather than divided out. Contained incidences are excluded by the Vandermonde transversality proof. Same-slope collisions are bounded by two. Common affine color normalization is bijective and cannot lower the numerator.

This is not a plan. The next full-solve bridge is the exact lemma

L-CYCLE88-464-U-OFFICIAL-MCA-FAIL-TO-PROXIMITY-PRIZE-BRIDGE

binding this explicit fail row to the final prize quantifiers or constructing the corresponding official challenge/adversary packet.

20260621_cycle87_explicit_separator_context(5).zip
Zip Archive
# COMMON PROMPT FOR CYCLE 87

Try to fully solve the problem. If you cannot fully solve it, progress it as
much as possible. No Internet. Take all the time to reason you need. Use MAX
reasoning.

You are working on the RS-MCA / proximity-prize finite obstruction route. Do
not brainstorm from scratch. Read the attached context first, especially:

- CYCLE87_CURRENT_STATE.md
- m1_cycle86_two_copy_affine_color_returns_audit.md
- m1_cycle85_role05_transfer_returns_audit.md
- m1_cycle84_wallbreaker_returns_audit.md
- the Cycle86 raw returns if you need to inspect a worker's exact claim

The active target is:


text
L-CYCLE86-EXPLICIT-TWO-COPY-SEPARATOR-CERTIFICATE


Prove, with a replayable certificate, that a concrete separator y for the
two-copy construction has projective multiplicity at most 8 and that the
resulting supports form one transverse affine syndrome line of a single
official-rate GRS code.

Equivalent checker wall:
